title: eremophila metallicorum
intro: Eremophila metallicorum, also known as Western Gold Dust Bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the figwort family and is prized for its vibrant yellow flowers and attractive foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, and its leaves have been found to contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and soothe skin irritations. Eremophila metallicorum can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to offer health benefits including immune system support and antioxidant activity.
